task_completion.description: Create the first graph titled 'Fleet CPU vs SLA Target' with the peak fleet CPU metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph with the exact title 'Fleet CPU vs SLA Target'. The graph's legend or metric input area must show the metric 'maxSeries(servers.ec2_instance_*.cpu.*)'.

task_completion.description: Add the 80% SLA ceiling line to the first graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Fleet CPU vs SLA Target' graph. The legend must show 'threshold(80,"SLA Limit 80%")' and there should be a visible horizontal line at the 80 mark on the Y-axis.

task_completion.description: Create the second graph titled 'SLA Breach Windows' with the nested removeBelowValue function.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a second graph with the exact title 'SLA Breach Windows'. The legend or metric input must show the exact nested function: 'removeBelowValue(maxSeries(servers.ec2_instance_*.cpu.*), 80)'.

task_completion.description: Create the third graph titled 'Database CPU vs SLA Target' with the RDS database CPU metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a third graph with the exact title 'Database CPU vs SLA Target'. The legend or metric input area must show the metric 'servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization'.

task_completion.description: Add the 90% DB SLA ceiling line to the third graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Database CPU vs SLA Target' graph. The legend must show 'threshold(90,"DB SLA Limit 90%")' and there should be a visible horizontal line at the 90 mark on the Y-axis.

task_completion.description: Save the dashboard with the exact name 'SLA Compliance Report'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the top of the Graphite dashboard interface, the URL, or a save confirmation dialog. The dashboard name must be exactly 'SLA Compliance Report'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Graphite web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Graphite web UI (e.g., dashboard view, metric composer), not a text editor, script, or terminal window.

integrity.description: The metrics and graphs are genuinely rendered by the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should display actual plotted data lines (or empty graph grids if no data exists for the time range) and standard Graphite UI elements, rather than pasted text or mocked images.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the dashboard name or graph titles.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI should look standard without the browser developer console open to edit HTML elements directly.
